title: Quantitative Mertens lower bound for the prime reciprocal sum

source_refs: Erdős-647 campaign (Mnehmos/llm-driven-proof-search), Layer A quantitative-Mertens infrastructure; classical: Mertens (1874)

informal_statement: For every real x ≥ 2, log 2 · log log x − (1/2 + log 2 · log log 2 + (1 + 1/log 2)(1 + 2√2)) ≤ ∑_{p≤x} 1/p: an unconditional, fully explicit quantitative form of Mertens' second theorem (with leading constant log 2 in place of the optimal 1).
informal_proof_idea: Substitute the real-valued Chebyshev lower bound for θ pointwise into the exact Mertens identity, split the weighted integral by linearity into the closed-form main term, the closed-form weight integral, and the two explicitly bounded error integrals, then collect constants.
formal_statement_pp:
theorem prime_reciprocal_quantitative_lower :
    ∀ x : ℝ, 2 ≤ x → Real.log 2 * Real.log (Real.log x) -
      (1/2 + Real.log 2 * Real.log (Real.log 2) + (1 + (Real.log 2)⁻¹) * (1 + 2*Real.sqrt 2))
      ≤ ∑ p ∈ Finset.Icc 1 ⌊x⌋₊ with p.Prime, (1/(p:ℝ))

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Neutral Prime Distribution port (mathcorpus #12 / proofsearch #253) of Erdős-647 campaign theorem erdos647_mertens_assembly (problem_version 15d4503a-0985-4d50-9c6b-aa445348bb9d). Statement and proof are the kernel-verified campaign snapshot verbatim modulo the neutral rename; proof authority is the Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop.
